dict.string:
Life Agent — ONE personal agent that fuses the signals only Shortcuts can read.
THE EDGE: a cloud agent (OpenClaw, Hermes, etc.) can read your files and shell — but it can read NONE of the things below. These are device-only and permissioned, and Shortcuts is the one sanctioned place a normal user can pull them all together: • Health: latest heart rate, sleep, steps (HealthKit) • Calendar: what's next today • Location: where you are now • Focus + battery: your current context and device state
WHAT IT DOES: 1. Reads heart rate + today's events + location + Focus + battery. 2. Sends that context to a model: "Given my body, schedule, and context right now, what are the 3 best things to do next?" 3. Shows the answer and logs it to a "Life Agent" note.
HONEST BOUNDARY: every signal is read with YOUR explicit permission, on-device. The agent proposes — it doesn't act on your behalf or send data anywhere except the model call you configured. It reads a lot, which is why this shortcut scores low on safety: that's the point — you SEE everything it touches.
SETUP: grant Health/Location/Calendar when prompted; put your model endpoint/key in "Get Contents of URL" (or use the on-device "Use Model" action on iOS 27 to keep it fully private).
🧠 Given your body, schedule, and context right now — here's what to do next:
🧠 Your Life Agent has a plan.
